text: fat is 68.4g. polyunsaturated fat, saturated fat, and omega3 are doing most of the work. for nuts, fat quality has to justify the calorie density.
carbs is 13.1g. fibre is where things start to fall off. for a nut, fat quality matters a ton.
protein is 13.7g. protein quality is a real strength here. protein is support here, not the whole argument.
vitamin e at 6% DV stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
magnesium at 63% DV, zinc at 46% DV, iron at 31% DV stand out most. that gives this food real mineral support.

on the plus side. portable, practical snack food because nuts score real points in life because they are easy to use. fat and texture help satiety because they can hold up better than many snack foods. still a recognisable whole-food option because that keeps them cleaner than most processed snacks.

on the weaker side. calorie density is very high because that makes portion control a real issue. protein is useful but not elite because nuts help, but they are not protein stars. cost or allergy issues can limit use because practical downsides still count.
B tier. Best in small amounts where the fats and minerals matter more than sheer calories.
